LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NELSON TOLENTINO'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM UNDER DEFAULT UNDER CCP 473(B). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT HE EXERCISED SUCH REASONABLE DILIGENCE AS A MAN OF ORDINARY PRUDENCE USUALLY BESTOWS UPON IMPORTANT BUSINESS. NO SHOWING OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. CCP SECTION 473(B). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Feb-23-2011 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Sep-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935.

Robert F. Cohen, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Thomasen Morales, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Nelson C. Tolentino.
Default was entered against defendant on December 4, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed on July 10, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Thomasen Morales, and against the defaulted defendant Nelson C. Tolentino as follows:

Damages/Principal - $63,150.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $8,913.26.
Costs - $409.86, for a total judgment in the amount of $72,473.12.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-21-2009 Continued To Oct-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2009 Continued To Jul-31-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Under Default Under Ccp 473(B)
Off Calendar Apr-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NELSON TOLENTINO'S Motion for Relief From Under Default Under CCP 473(b). Appearances: Jeffrey P. Fleitman, Esq. of Law Offices of Jeffrey Fleitman, (310) 399-2889, Appearing thru Courtcall, appearing for Plaintiff; Yano Rubinstein, Esq. of the Rubinstein Law Group, (415) 277-1900, appearing for Defendant moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Defendant fails to show that he exercised such reasonable diligence as a man of ordinary prudence usually bestows upon important business. No showing of mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect. CCP section 473(b). (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2009 Continued To May-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212
